title: Bart Gets A Z

jokes.text: Check it out. That crazy lady's singing a million-year-old song.
jokes.setup: Edna singing along to an older song
jokes.punchline: Bart's typical exaggerated dismissal of anything not current
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart perspective - dismissive and hyperbolic about adult culture

jokes.text: Text 'Uncle'. Text 'Uncle'.
jokes.setup: Students ignoring teacher, playing with phones
jokes.punchline: Teacher surrendering like in physical fight but using modern texting terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever updating of classic phrase 'cry uncle' to modern texting context - excellent economy and structure

jokes.text: You're children. Why do you all need cell phones? Safety. Emergencies. Safety. Educational.
jokes.setup: Reasonable teacher question about kids having phones
jokes.punchline: Kids giving rehearsed, unconvincing justifications
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about kids' obvious rationalizations for wanting phones

jokes.text: Vibrating sound effect growing progressively louder
jokes.setup: Teacher asks students to put phones on vibrate
jokes.punchline: Collective vibrating creates overwhelming noise
jokes.explanation: Great escalation gag - teacher's reasonable request backfires in unexpected way

jokes.text: They're all going in the drawer with the biology frogs. (croaking)
jokes.setup: Teacher confiscating phones
jokes.punchline: Phones stored with living frogs, frogs respond
jokes.explanation: Surreal Simpsons-style gag - unexpected combination of technology and biology

jokes.text: My stroked-out grandfather has more memory than that thing.
jokes.setup: Old classroom computer being slow
jokes.punchline: Dark comparison to grandfather's stroke condition
jokes.explanation: Clever dark humor mixing technology complaints with medical tragedy - very Simpsons

jokes.text: How is he doing? Better. Thanks.
jokes.setup: Dark joke about grandfather's stroke
jokes.punchline: Casual, polite follow-up treating serious situation normally
jokes.explanation: Nice deadpan follow-up that makes the dark humor even more absurd through politeness

jokes.text: Okay. Now I'll be the dog, and you be the daddy. (barks)
jokes.setup: Homer drunk at home
jokes.punchline: Proposing bizarre role-playing game with family dog
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist Homer moment showing his drunk logic and relationship with pets

jokes.text: Hmm. You know, my dad is a lot more fun after a few beers.
jokes.setup: Bart observing drunk Homer being playful
jokes.punchline: Kid's matter-of-fact assessment of parental alcohol improvement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic and family dysfunction - casual acceptance of parental drinking as improvement

jokes.text: Put blood in it? No, booze.
jokes.setup: Bart suggests 'Irishing up' the coffee
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's innocent misunderstanding of the phrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse character moment - naive misinterpretation of adult euphemism

jokes.text: And that's why I found Good Night, Gorilla to be the perfect summer beach read. Two stars for you. And an extra, 'cause you're going to have a hard life.
jokes.setup: Teacher drunk, giving book reviews to students
jokes.punchline: Casual prediction of student's difficult future as bonus point
jokes.explanation: Dark humor mixing children's literature with brutal honesty about student prospects

jokes.text: Hey! You know why they call it a choir. Because 'choir' we listening to this crap?
jokes.setup: Drunk teacher at school performance
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun insulting the choir performance
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad pun that works because it shows drunk teacher's poor judgment

jokes.text: Give me one night, Dewey. I'll get you off Broadway.
jokes.setup: Drunk teacher propositioning someone
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo disguised as career advice
jokes.explanation: Double entendre playing on 'get off' - typical drunk inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: Booze only makes you do things you already wanted to do.
jokes.setup: Worrying about getting teacher too drunk
jokes.punchline: Kid dispensing surprisingly sophisticated wisdom about alcohol
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Bart showing unexpected wisdom while remaining in character

jokes.text: I'm queen of the world! Oh! (crashing)
jokes.setup: Drunk teacher feeling liberated
jokes.punchline: Immediate physical comedy punishment for hubris
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons physical comedy - immediate comeuppance for overconfidence

jokes.text: But right now, I don't feel much like follying.
jokes.setup: Mention of Faculty Follies performance
jokes.punchline: Skinner turning 'folly' into a verb while being uncharacteristically emotional
jokes.explanation: Good Skinner moment showing his awkward way of expressing emotion through wordplay

jokes.text: Our cell phones! Our Blueteeth! My tip calculator!
jokes.setup: Students excited about getting technology back
jokes.punchline: Escalating from phones to increasingly specific/silly tech dependencies
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke about tech dependency, ending on absurd specific item

jokes.text: You mean the L Man/D Dog flame wars.
jokes.setup: Reference to Lincoln-Douglas debates
jokes.punchline: Reframing historical debates as internet flame wars with silly nicknames
jokes.explanation: Excellent modern recontextualization of historical reference - clever and economical

jokes.text: No one even plays four-square anymore. They just gossip.
jokes.setup: Homer reporting gossip from four-square court
jokes.punchline: Casual observation that playground has become gossip hub
jokes.explanation: Nice observational humor about how social spaces evolve, delivered with Homer's casual tone

jokes.text: But this is just nonsense punctuation.
jokes.setup: New teacher looking at traditional rewards
jokes.punchline: Dismissing emoticons as meaningless symbols
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor about tech-savvy teacher dismissing analog communication

jokes.text: Where do I find the books, the booze and the broads?
jokes.setup: Edna pretending to be a college freshman
jokes.punchline: Middle-aged woman using outdated college stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Edna's disconnect from actual college culture

jokes.text: Hey, Dean, how about a little privacy? I've got to matriculate.
jokes.setup: Edna trying to sound like a college student
jokes.punchline: Using 'matriculate' as if it's something private/sexual
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay showing Edna's confusion about college terminology

jokes.text: Honey, if we increase your endowment any more, we'll all be in the shade.
jokes.setup: Character in movie talking about college fundraising
jokes.punchline: Double entendre using 'endowment' in sexual context
jokes.explanation: Standard double entendre that serves to show what Edna finds funny in her low state

jokes.text: Stu is about more than just disco. I'm also-- ha! -- super Christian!
jokes.setup: Disco Stu trying to defend himself as potential partner
jokes.punchline: Adding religion as if it makes him more appealing
jokes.explanation: Good Disco Stu character moment - absurd combination of disco and Christianity

jokes.text: You're the first teacher that ever got me. (crying) Let it out. Let it out. (sobbing)
jokes.setup: Parent-teacher conference going well
jokes.punchline: Homer having emotional breakdown over basic teacher kindness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - over-emotional response to minimal kindness

jokes.text: Keep it in. Keep it in. (very loud sobbing)
jokes.setup: Teacher trying to calm Homer's crying
jokes.punchline: Advice backfires spectacularly with louder crying
jokes.explanation: Good escalation gag - reasonable advice making situation much worse

jokes.text: I ain't paid for Doris Kearns Goodwin in my life, and I ain't gonna start now.
jokes.setup: Character sitting in bookstore
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to not paying for presidential historian's books
jokes.explanation: Great Comic Book Guy moment - specific cultural reference with his characteristic freeloading

jokes.text: I believe you boys are looking for... The Answer.
jokes.setup: Boys looking for help in bookstore
jokes.punchline: Employee presenting generic self-help book as solution
jokes.explanation: Good setup for self-help book parody that drives the rest of the episode

jokes.text: where it was found by Pat Sajak
jokes.setup: Epic historical journey of 'The Answer' through great civilizations
jokes.punchline: Anticlimatic ending with game show host
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anticlimax - builds up ancient mystical lineage then deflates with mundane TV personality

jokes.text: But we do understand electricity. Every positively charged nucleus is...
jokes.setup: Self-help book claiming we don't understand electricity
jokes.punchline: Teacher immediately demonstrating we do understand it
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - teacher can't help but correct false information even when depressed

jokes.text: Cut it out. You're not a teacher anymore.
jokes.setup: Edna starting to teach
jokes.punchline: Bart brutally reminding her she's been fired
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart moment - unintentionally cruel but honest observation

jokes.text: What did he want? Beats me. I just gave him a banana.
jokes.setup: Customer interaction at muffin store
jokes.punchline: Complete disconnect between what customer wanted and what he got
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment showing Edna's incompetence at her new job

jokes.text: No matter what you've done, I always thought there was a spark of decency in you, Bart Simpson, but I was wrong. I never thought I'd say this to a child, but you are bad on the inside.
jokes.setup: Teacher confronting student who ruined her life
jokes.punchline: Adult telling child they're fundamentally evil
jokes.explanation: Devastating character moment that subverts typical teacher-student dynamics - genuinely shocking

jokes.text: No... but the layers of bad on your surface go almost all the way to the center.
jokes.setup: Bart asking if he's bad inside
jokes.punchline: Homer's attempt at reassurance that's actually worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - trying to be supportive but making it worse through honest assessment

jokes.text: Kernels are kind of big.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about kernel of good inside
jokes.punchline: Homer taking metaphor literally and finding it inadequate
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer moment - literal interpretation destroying son's hope for redemption

jokes.text: Hilary Swank didn't graduate high school. She won two Oscars. And one of them she deserved.
jokes.setup: Drunk teacher telling students school doesn't matter
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about Hilary Swank's acting
jokes.explanation: Great throwaway line that manages to insult both education and Hilary Swank simultaneously

jokes.text: Oh, they're gonna eat you alive at Cal Tech, boy. Alive!
jokes.setup: Student asking about extra credit
jokes.punchline: Drunk teacher's brutal assessment of student's future
jokes.explanation: Drunk teacher's dramatic overreaction to simple question - repetition for emphasis

jokes.text: But it's stale. Eat the muffin.
jokes.setup: Student complaining about muffin quality
jokes.punchline: Edna's firm insistence despite valid complaint
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Edna's failed business - petty revenge through forced consumption

tmdb_overview: The fourth grade students of Springfield Elementary School decide to spike Ms. Krabappel's coffee in order to teach her a lesson after she takes away their cell phones. She is fired by Principal Skinner, who hires a new teacher named Zachary Vaughn. Although Vaughn is a hip young teacher who impresses the students, Bart is plagued by guilt and tries to get Ms. Krabappel hired back.
